input: I want a personal planning tool where I enter my seasonal activities and track how they change over time. Just a simple form for me — nobody else needs to see it.
I want a simple drawing canvas where I can sketch ideas with basic shapes and colors. Save my work locally, export as PNG. Private creative tool.
Build me a daily step counter that logs my walks and shows weekly trends as a line chart. Completely private health data.
I need a simple unit converter — type a number, pick the units, get the result. Metric to imperial and back. No accounts, no data saved.

I want an ordered reading list — books queued in the order I plan to read them. Mark each as want-to-read, reading, or finished to track my progress. Sort by author or date added, filter by reading status. Private to me.

Personal expense collection organized by spending category — dining, transport, entertainment. View spending trends over time as bar charts, filter by date or category, sort by amount. All data stays on my device.

I already have a garden planner module (contentType: tools, structure: form, scale: 2, boundary: none). Now I want to add a seed inventory — a list of all my seeds sorted by planting season, filterable by crop type. It should group with my planner when I view my garden workspace.

I have a step counter (contentType: health, structure: form, mechanics: [track, chart], boundary: none, scale: 2). I want to add a nutrition log that tracks daily meals and shows calorie trends as charts. No filtering or sorting needed — just log and visualize. It should auto-group with my step counter since both are health data.

I have a social feed module (contentType: social, structure: feed, mechanics: [like, follow, share, post], boundary: ask, scale: 3). I want to add a user profile — a block that composes bio info, avatar, and an activity stream into an interactive unit. The profile's only interaction mechanic is following (subscribing to updates) — likes, shares, and posts happen on the feed, not the profile. Profiles shouldn't auto-group with feed content.

I have three S2 tools modules: a garden planner, a seed inventory, and a calendar — all contentType: tools, boundary: none. I want an S3 block that pools them into a single workspace where I can filter across all three by category.

I'm running an education-study module (contentType: education-study, structure: form, mechanics: [track], boundary: none, scale: 3) where individual students complete reading reflections. I want to add a standalone education-discussion community — a self-contained module where students create discussion topics, reply to each other in nested threads, and vote on the best contributions. Students must sign up. This education-discussion module must NOT auto-group with education-study modules.

I have personal tools modules (boundary: none, scale: 2). My partner and I want a shared family collection at Module-scale (S5) — an unordered group where we each contribute items, post new things, and share selected items from our private modules. Only two mechanics: post and share. Consent required before anything leaves our private modules.

My planning tool started as private (contentType: tools, structure: form, mechanics: [track], boundary: none, scale: 2). Now I want to make it publicly accessible so others can see and share links to my plans. Not charging anyone — freely accessible. Keep the same contentType, structure, and scale — only update boundary and mechanics. What are the updated MSS tags?

My recipe collection (contentType: tools, structure: collection, mechanics: [filter, sort], boundary: none, scale: 2) has grown and friends keep asking to see it. I'll let people browse and share their favorites with others, but I want to approve each person before they can access. The collection itself stays the same size and shape — same scale, same structure. What are the updated tags?

My personal art portfolio (contentType: art, structure: collection, mechanics: [filter, sort], boundary: none, scale: 2) is ready for public display. I want to transform it into a ranked exhibition — visitors vote on pieces, award gold to favorites, and share links to works they love. The old filter and sort mechanics are gone — replaced by a ranked feed with voting, awards, and sharing. Open to everyone without signup. This grows from a personal collection into a standalone community exhibition module.

Our community health dashboard (contentType: health, structure: collection, mechanics: [track, chart, filter], boundary: ask, scale: 3) has attracted researchers who want to study anonymized aggregate trends. We're creating a research platform with institutional access tiers — institutions pay for access to the aggregated longitudinal data pool with filtering, sorting, and visualization of pre-existing data. What MSS tags describe this research platform?

A farmer wants to register their produce stand on a farmers market network. The stand has categorized seasonal produce that customers can browse, sort by price, and filter by category. The farmer shares inventory data publicly. What MSS tags should the stand's Agent Card declare?

A Block-scale (S3) social feed module needs an Agent Card to join a larger social network. The feed is algorithm-sorted with exactly four mechanics: users like posts, follow accounts, share posts, and create new content (post). No voting or other mechanics. Users must consent before their data is shared. What MSS tags should the feed module's Agent Card declare?

A personal health dashboard (not a research tool — it tracks individual vitals) wants to join a research network. The dashboard is a block that organizes multiple health metric groups with charts showing trends over time and filtering by metric type. Users must give consent before health data is shared. What MSS tags does the dashboard's own Agent Card need?

A farmers market (contentType: produce, boundary: all, scale: 6) discovers that clothing stalls, craft vendors, and food trucks have joined alongside produce stands. The market needs to promote to a general commerce cluster at the same scale (S6) — organizing vendors in nested categories (produce, clothing, food). Vendors register to join but shoppers browse freely. Shoppers can sort by category, filter vendors, and vote on favorites. What are the promoted cluster's MSS tags?
